title: Cambridge Challenge
seoDescription: Take a fast Cambridge-style reasoning quiz with staged visual logic, number patterns, deduction, and analytical puzzles.
pageTitle: Only 4% Can Pass This Cambridge Test
summary: A supervision-style reasoning challenge for sharp minds.

homepage.title: Can You Pass This Cambridge Entrance Test?
homepage.summary: A supervision-style reasoning challenge for sharp minds.

infoPanel.title: About This Cambridge Test
infoPanel.intro: This Cambridge-style quiz is a fast reasoning challenge inspired by the kind of clear thinking, pattern recognition, deduction, and careful reading often associated with college interviews and supervision-style discussion. It is not an official University of Cambridge exam, but it is designed to feel sharp, focused, and mentally demanding.

columns.title: How It Works
columns.body: The quiz moves through five escalating stages: Warm Up, Challenge Round, Expert Round, Impossible Round, and Final 3%. Each short stage mixes visual, number, logic, deduction, pattern, attention, and lateral-style reasoning so the challenge feels varied while steadily becoming harder.

columns.title: About Cambridge Admissions
columns.body: Cambridge undergraduate admissions are competitive and course-specific. Depending on the subject, applicants may be assessed through academic achievement, written work, admissions tests, interviews, references, and personal statements. There is no single universal Cambridge entrance exam for every applicant.

infoPanel.footerBody: Every question in this quiz has one correct answer and an explanation. Your score is for entertainment and self-challenge only; it should not be treated as an official admissions result or academic assessment.

landing.challengeText: Most players never reach the final round.

profiles.title: You beat the test.
profiles.copy: Exceptional reasoning across the full challenge. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Elite reasoning.
profiles.copy: You stayed sharp through the harder rounds. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Sharp under pressure.
profiles.copy: Strong performance across the skill rounds, led by {stage}.

profiles.title: Above-average reasoning.
profiles.copy: You showed good instincts, especially in {stage}.

profiles.title: Solid score.
profiles.copy: You handled several rounds well. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Keep sharpening.
profiles.copy: The hardest rounds reward calm pattern memory. Your best area was {stage}.

stageGroups.title: Warm Up

questions.prompt: Which item is different from the others?
questions.explanation: Circle, square, and triangle are shapes. Number is not a shape.

questions.prompt: Complete the doubling pattern.
questions.explanation: Each number doubles: 2, 4, 8, 16, then 32.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2   4   8   16   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which number must be divisible by 5?
questions.explanation: Numbers ending in 0 or 5 are divisible by 5.

questions.prompt: Which group has exactly four dots?
questions.explanation: Group B contains exactly four dots.

questions.prompt: Which shape has five sides?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has five sides.

questions.prompt: Which symbol is shown twice?
questions.explanation: The circle appears twice.

questions.prompt: All zems are blue. Item A is a zem. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If all zems are blue and A is a zem, A must be blue.

questions.prompt: Which value is greatest?
questions.explanation: 41 is the largest number.

stageGroups.encouragement: You cleared the warm-up. Next comes the challenge round, where the patterns get quicker.

stageGroups.title: Challenge Round

questions.prompt: Which option completes the fill pattern?
questions.explanation: The same square alternates black, white, black, then white.

questions.prompt: Which shape is the visual exception?
questions.explanation: A, B, and C are circles. D is a square.

questions.prompt: Which symbol should appear in the empty place?
questions.explanation: The row mirrors around the center, so the final symbol matches the first.

questions.prompt: Which number completes the vertical count rule?
questions.explanation: The counts go 2, 4, 6, then 8.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2   4   6   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which symbol appears most often?
questions.explanation: Triangle appears more often than any other symbol.

questions.prompt: Which group contains one more item than Group B?
questions.explanation: Group B has three items. Group C has four.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the corner pattern?
questions.explanation: The marked corner moves clockwise: top left, top right, then bottom right.

questions.prompt: Which value is exactly halfway between 20 and 40?
questions.explanation: 30 is halfway between 20 and 40.

stageGroups.encouragement: Good pace. The expert round now asks for slower, more deliberate thinking.

stageGroups.title: Expert Round

questions.prompt: Which square number comes next?
questions.explanation: The sequence is 3², 4², 5², 6², then 7².
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">9   16   25   36   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the next prime number.
questions.explanation: The prime sequence continues with 23.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">5   7   11   13   17   19   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which value completes the growing difference pattern?
questions.explanation: The differences are +2, +4, +6, +8, then +10.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">11   13   17   23   31   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What number finishes the row rule?
questions.explanation: The third number is the first number multiplied by the second: 5 × 6 = 30.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2×3=6   4×5=20   5×6=?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If 5 items cost 35 units, what do 8 items cost?
questions.explanation: Each item costs 7 units, so 8 items cost 56.

questions.prompt: The average of 10, 14, and x is 18. What is x?
questions.explanation: The total must be 54. 10 + 14 = 24, so x = 30.

questions.prompt: A value rises from 80 to 100. What is the percentage increase?
questions.explanation: The increase is 20 out of 80, which is 25%.

questions.prompt: Which number completes the n times n plus one pattern?
questions.explanation: The terms are n × (n + 1): 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, then 42.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2   6   12   20   30   ?</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: You reached expert territory. The impossible round is built to stop most players.

stageGroups.title: Impossible Round

questions.prompt: All drens are faln. No faln are kets. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If every dren is faln and no faln are kets, no drens can be kets.

questions.prompt: The more you remove from it, the larger it becomes. What is it?
questions.explanation: Removing material can make a hole larger.

questions.prompt: A container has 12 items. Half are removed, then half of the rest are removed. How many remain?
questions.explanation: After removing half, 6 remain. Removing half of 6 leaves 3.

questions.prompt: Only people with tokens may enter. Mira entered. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only people with tokens may enter, Mira must have had a token.

questions.prompt: A pad and marker cost 1.10 units. The pad costs 1.00 more. What does the marker cost?
questions.explanation: The marker is 0.05 and the pad is 1.05, making 1.10 in total.

questions.prompt: Which conclusion is safest? The sample increased after the change.
questions.explanation: May have helped is cautious and stays within the evidence.

questions.prompt: A line has two ends. It is cut once. How many line pieces are there?
questions.explanation: One cut creates two pieces.

questions.prompt: If every correct answer is checked, and this answer was not checked, what must be true?
questions.explanation: If every correct answer is checked, an unchecked answer cannot be correct.

stageGroups.encouragement: Final stretch. The last stage is the 4% Cambridge challenge.

questions.prompt: Which term completes the triangular number pattern?
questions.explanation: The triangular numbers increase by adding 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, then 7.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">1   3   6   10   15   21   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Some vels are nors. All nors are trins. What must be true?
questions.explanation: The vels that are nors must also be trins.

questions.prompt: Only diagrams with labels are scored. This diagram was scored. What follows?
questions.explanation: If only labelled diagrams are scored, a scored diagram must have labels.

questions.prompt: Which value completes the difference-of-squares rule?
questions.explanation: The rule is n² minus (n−1)². For 8²−7², the result is 15.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="48" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">4²−3²=7   5²−4²=9   6²−5²=11   7²−6²=13   8²−7²=?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A is left of B. C is right of D. D is right of B. Which must be leftmost?
questions.explanation: A is left of B, while D and C are to the right of B.

questions.prompt: Which symbol completes the two-step cycle?
questions.explanation: The cycle moves circle, triangle, square, diamond, then repeats.

questions.prompt: If every approved answer is reviewed, and this answer was not reviewed, what follows?
questions.explanation: An approved answer would have been reviewed, so an unreviewed answer was not approved.

questions.prompt: A number is multiplied by 3, then 6 is added, giving 33. What was the number?
questions.explanation: Subtract 6 to get 27, then divide by 3 to get 9.
